ontology.foodex2_description: Choux pastry / cream puff — pastry filled with cream
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No dedicated FoodEx2 code for mini cream puff; A0FKP (choux pastry) is the closest defensible analogous anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for cream puff; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One mini cream puff weighs approximately 20 g, representing a single bite-sized serving as commonly consumed as a snack or dessert item.

nutrient_source.title: Mini Cream Puff — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mini Cream Puff (F11110018). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream puff entry (FDC ID 172762) as HPB does not report fatty acid sub-fractions; see notes.

gi_source.title: Cream puff, choux pastry — GI analogous estimate based on refined wheat pastry products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for mini cream puff in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: refined wheat choux pastry, consistent with GI values reported for refined wheat-based pastry products (GI range 60–70) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (4.51 g per 20 g serving) / 100 = 2.93, rounded to 3.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High saturated fat (14.5 g/100 g) and moderate sodium (175 mg/100 g) with trace trans fat
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of mini cream puffs due to very high saturated fat content (~14.5 g/100 g from butter and whipping cream) which contributes to arterial stiffness, combined with moderate sodium (175 mg/100 g) and trace trans fat that further impairs endothelial function; limit to rare occasional treats only.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined choux pastry with medium-high GI (~65), 22.6 g carbohydrate/100 g, low fibre (1.67 g/100 g), and high fat delaying but not preventing postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should limit mini cream puffs to rare occasional consumption only, as the refined choux pastry has a medium-to-high glycaemic index (~65), 22.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1.67 g dietary fibre, and the high fat content from cream delays but does not prevent significant postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very high saturated fat (~14.5 g/100 g) and trace trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) from butter and whipping cream
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of mini cream puffs due to very high saturated fat (~14.5 g/100 g) and trace trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) from butter and whipping cream, both of which raise LDL cholesterol and increase atherosclerotic risk; this food is not suitable for heart-healthy dietary patterns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mini cream puff is a bite-sized Western pastry of French origin, made from choux pastry (pâte à choux) — a light, hollow dough prepared by cooking flour, butter, water, and eggs together, then piping and baking until puffed and golden. Each small shell is filled with sweetened whipping cream or pastry cream. The texture is a contrast of a crisp, thin outer shell and a rich, airy cream interior. Mini cream puffs are widely consumed in Singapore as a snack or dessert item, often sold in bakeries and supermarkets. Nutritionally, they are energy-dense at 337 kcal/100 g, with high total fat (24.85 g/100 g) dominated by saturated fat (~14.5 g/100 g from dairy cream and butter), moderate carbohydrate (22.56 g/100 g) from refined wheat flour and added sugar, and relatively low dietary fibre (1.67 g/100 g). The refined pastry base has a medium-to-high glycaemic index (~65), making this food unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Allergens include gluten, wheat, eggs, and dairy. The food is vegetarian but not vegan.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a mini cream puff?
How much fat is in a cream puff?
Is cream puff suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of cream puff?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11110018) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream puff entry as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; this reduces saturated fat confidence to medium but does not affect overall record confidence given the primary macro integrity. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database category.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (14.5 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for cream puff (FDC ID 172762), as HPB Singapore Foodo does not report fatty acid sub-fractions for this item. This is documented as an adapted source for these two fields. 2. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient and fatty acid sub-fraction data for mini cream puff are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11110018) or the USDA FoodData Central cream puff entry (FDC ID 172762) as of 2026: monounsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid sub-fraction not reported by HPB or USDA for this composite product; polyunsaturated_fat_g — fatty acid sub-fraction not reported by HPB or USDA for this composite product; potassium_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018 and not available in USDA FDC 172762 for this specific formulation; cholesterol_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_c_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018, negligible expected in baked pastry; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_b6_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_b12_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; folate_mcg_dfe — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_d_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; vitamin_e_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; phosphorus_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; magnesium_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; zinc_mg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; selenium_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018; water_g — moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo entry F11110018. Sources consulted and confirmed unavailability: HPB Singapore Foodo (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/, accessed 2026-04-16) and USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/, FDC ID 172762, accessed 2026-04-16). 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat choux pastry products in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for mini cream puff was found. 4. Mini cream puff is a Western-origin food (French choux pastry tradition) widely sold in Singapore; it does not require Singaporean multilingual naming as it is not a Singaporean dish. No mandatory local-language names apply under G6. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical commercial formulation using halal-certified dairy and no pork-derived gelatin; this should be verified against specific product labelling.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Updated ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent'. Added general -1 sentinel justification to notes.
